items.title: Jefferies Upgrades Royal Gold For Compelling Growth Potential
items.author: Stjepan Kalinic
items.description: Jefferies has upgraded Royal Gold to a Buy rating, citing the company's strong assets, rising production, and a 12.5% dividend increase. The bank sees compelling growth potential from the streaming and royalty business leader for precious metals.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Jefferies has upgraded Royal Gold to a Buy rating, citing the company's strong assets, rising production, and a 12.5% dividend increase. The bank sees compelling growth potential from the streaming and royalty business leader for precious metals.

The article mentions that Royal Gold's growth is driven by increased throughput at the Mount Milligan mine in Canada and the ongoing ramp-up at the Barrick Gold's Pueblo Viejo mine in the Dominican Republic.
The article mentions that Goldman Sachs had a more optimistic $3,000 price target for gold in 2025, but recently moved the goalpost, seeing the price reach that level in mid-2026.

items.title: These Were the 5 Top-Performing Stocks in the Dow Jones Industrial Average in 2024
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The article discusses the top-performing Dow Jones stocks of 2024, including Nvidia, Walmart, American Express, and Goldman Sachs. It highlights the factors that contributed to their strong performance, such as AI enthusiasm, e-commerce growth, and consistent earnings growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nvidia was the best-performing Dow Jones stock in 2024, benefiting from enthusiasm surrounding artificial intelligence (AI).
Walmart's e-commerce initiatives showed success in 2024, with strong comparable sales growth in the U.S. and international businesses.
American Express remained a prominent position in the Berkshire Hathaway portfolio, providing investors with reasons to buy the stock.
Goldman Sachs achieved consistent year-over-year earnings-per-share growth, which was one of the reasons why investors chose to buy the banking stock.
